mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate compresses the equity discount rate and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Low FEDFUNDS months do not produce statistically higher 20-day returns than high-rate months over a 5-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A labor market deterioration captured by UNRATE rising more than 0.3 percentage points within three months signals falling household income and declining consumption, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in three months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reveals broad demand collapse that is stronger than either sector alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint negative momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce negative 20-day returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25 percent, positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) signals effective monetary stimulus transmission to real output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent, positive INDPRO momentum coincides with positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) confirm a sustained supply-side contraction that reduces earnings visibility and pushes the S&P 500 lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS) and stable consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) indicate non-inflationary growth that supports equity multiple expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Jointly rising PAYEMS and stable CPIAUCSL fail to produce positive 5-day returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1000 thousand units triggers a residential investment contraction that spills over into broader demand weakness, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below 1000 coincides with positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A higher level of real GDP (GDPC1) reflects aggregate demand strength that underpins corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 and 60-day forward returns is not positive over any 5-year rolling window.
